proofs.proof_tex:
For gamma=0, def:exponents gives beta_{alpha,0}=0 and therefore r_star(alpha,0)=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha). The gamma=0 clause of def:feasible-rate chooses a fixed clip below the strict-overlap floor and sets r_feas=r_star, so equality holds at the strict-overlap endpoint.

Now let gamma>0. By def:feasible-rate, with kappa=2(1+alpha)/(2+alpha) and zeta(a,c,gamma)=sup_{lambda>=0} min{1/2-lambda/2, c-lambda, a+lambda/(2 gamma)}, the feasible exponent is
  r_feas(alpha,gamma,a,c)=min{r_star(alpha,gamma), kappa zeta(a,c,gamma)}.
Hence r_feas<=r_star identically. Moreover
  r_star-r_feas=max{0, r_star-kappa zeta(a,c,gamma)}.
Thus the rate gap is positive exactly on the nuisance-limited weak-overlap branch kappa zeta(a,c,gamma)<r_star(alpha,gamma), and it is zero whenever kappa zeta(a,c,gamma)>=r_star(alpha,gamma). This includes gamma=0 by the preceding paragraph. No additional stochastic argument is used; this is only the algebra of the constructed exponent.

Let Pclass(alpha,gamma) denote the law class in def:law-class, augmented for the feasible upper analysis by the estimator-side hypotheses used in oeq:feasible-upper: ass:marginal-overlap, ass:nuisance-rate, ass:vc-localized-envelope, and, at gamma=0, ass:strict-overlap-endpoint, together with the standing provisional bounded-score and polynomial-nuisance-exponent conditions recorded in the design block. The policy class is assumed rich enough to contain the two witness-optimal policies required by thm:minimax-lower.

Lower bound. Under ass:margin-window and the richness condition on Pi, thm:minimax-lower gives constants c>0 and n0 such that for all n>=n0,
  M_n(alpha,gamma) >= c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)}.
By def:exponents, beta_{alpha,gamma}=0 if gamma=0 and beta_{alpha,gamma}=alpha gamma/(alpha+1) if gamma>0, D_{alpha,gamma}=2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}, and
  r_star(alpha,gamma)=(1+alpha)/D_{alpha,gamma}=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}).
This is the sharp converse exponent supplied by the calibrated two-point construction.

Upper bound. The node oeq:feasible-upper constructs the cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM of def:feasible-erm with the clip chosen by the bias-variance calculation in def:feasible-rate. It proves that there exist constants C,p>0 such that, for all large n,
  sup_{P in Pclass(alpha,gamma)} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma)} (log n)^p.
Since M_n(alpha,gamma) is the infimum over all measurable Pi-valued estimators of this same worst-case regret, evaluating the infimum at the particular estimator pi_hat_n gives
  M_n(alpha,gamma) <= sup_{P in Pclass(alpha,gamma)} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma)} (log n)^p.
Combining this display with the lower bound yields the claimed two-sided bracket
  c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma)} (log n)^p.

Exponent comparison and endpoint. By lem:rate-gap-branch, r_feas<=r_star always, and at gamma=0 the strict-overlap endpoint reduces to
  r_feas(alpha,0)=r_star(alpha,0)=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha).
For gamma>0 the same lemma shows the precise phase interpretation: the gap r_star-r_feas is positive on the nuisance-limited branch kappa zeta(a,c,gamma)<r_star(alpha,gamma), and it vanishes when the available nuisance rates are strong enough that kappa zeta(a,c,gamma)>=r_star(alpha,gamma). Therefore the bracket is tight up to logarithms at gamma=0 and in every equal-exponent branch, while the strict-gap weak-overlap branch is the residual feasible-tightness question recorded in oeq:feasible-tight.

Interpretation. The lower inequality is an information-theoretic minimax converse for offline policy welfare regret under one-sided polynomial overlap decay and a Tsybakov margin. The upper inequality is attained by the explicit feasible cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM with the derived clipping rule. The result is therefore an offline weak-overlap welfare-regret rate bracket extending the strict-overlap policy-learning rate; unlike online diminishing-exploration regret bounds, the target here is the fixed-population welfare-regret functional M_n(alpha,gamma).

Assume the parameter and nuisance-rate regime satisfies r_feas(alpha,gamma)=r_star(alpha,gamma). By thm:rate-characterization, the cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM of def:feasible-erm, with the clip selected in oeq:feasible-upper, obeys
  sup_{P in Pclass(alpha,gamma)} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma)} (log n)^p.
Substituting r_feas=r_star gives
  sup_{P in Pclass(alpha,gamma)} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} (log n)^p.
The lower side of thm:rate-characterization gives M_n(alpha,gamma)>=c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} for all measurable Pi-valued estimators. Hence no estimator can improve the polynomial exponent beyond r_star, while the feasible cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM attains that exponent up to logarithmic factors. By lem:rate-gap-branch, gamma=0 is one such equal-exponent regime, with r_star(alpha,0)=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha).

added_lemmas.statement: Under def:feasible-rate and def:exponents, r_feas(alpha,gamma)<=r_star(alpha,gamma). At gamma=0, r_feas=r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha). For gamma>0, writing r_feas=min{r_star, kappa zeta(a,c,gamma)}, the gap r_star-r_feas is positive exactly when kappa zeta(a,c,gamma)<r_star(alpha,gamma), and is zero otherwise.
added_lemmas.proof_tex:
For gamma=0, def:exponents gives beta_{alpha,0}=0 and therefore r_star(alpha,0)=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha). The gamma=0 clause of def:feasible-rate chooses a fixed clip below the strict-overlap floor and sets r_feas=r_star, so equality holds at the strict-overlap endpoint.

Now let gamma>0. By def:feasible-rate, with kappa=2(1+alpha)/(2+alpha) and zeta(a,c,gamma)=sup_{lambda>=0} min{1/2-lambda/2, c-lambda, a+lambda/(2 gamma)}, the feasible exponent is

Hence r_feas<=r_star identically. Moreover
  r_star-r_feas=max{0, r_star-kappa zeta(a,c,gamma)}.

This includes gamma=0 by the preceding paragraph. No additional stochastic argument is used; this is only the algebra of the constructed exponent.

added_lemmas.statement: Under thm:rate-characterization, in every parameter and nuisance-rate regime where r_feas(alpha,gamma)=r_star(alpha,gamma), the cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM of def:feasible-erm attains the converse welfare-regret exponent r_star(alpha,gamma) up to logarithmic factors. In particular this holds at gamma=0.
added_lemmas.proof_tex:
Assume the parameter and nuisance-rate regime satisfies r_feas(alpha,gamma)=r_star(alpha,gamma). By thm:rate-characterization, the cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM of def:feasible-erm, with the clip selected in oeq:feasible-upper, obeys
  sup_{P in Pclass(alpha,gamma)} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma)} (log n)^p.
Substituting r_feas=r_star gives
  sup_{P in Pclass(alpha,gamma)} E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} (log n)^p.
The lower side of thm:rate-characterization gives M_n(alpha,gamma)>=c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} for all measurable Pi-valued estimators. Hence no estimator can improve the polynomial exponent beyond r_star, while the feasible cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM attains that exponent up to logarithmic factors. By lem:rate-gap-branch, gamma=0 is one such equal-exponent regime, with r_star(alpha,0)=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha).

proposed_statement_changes.current: Under ass:margin-window, ass:marginal-overlap, ass:nuisance-rate, Pi rich (witness-optimal policies in Pi), gamma<=alpha+1: c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma)} (log n)^p, with r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}) (sharp converse, thm:minimax-lower, all gamma) and r_feas the DERIVED plain-clipped-AIPW exponent (oeq:feasible-upper), r_feas<=r_star with equality at gamma=0. FIRST welfare-regret rate under weak (one-sided polynomial) overlap, extending strict-overlap policy learning. The r_star-r_feas gap (positive for gamma>0) is the weak-overlap nuisance-estimation cost; tightness is open (oeq:feasible-tight).
proposed_statement_changes.proposed: Under ass:margin-window, ass:marginal-overlap, ass:nuisance-rate, the standing feasible-estimator conditions used in oeq:feasible-upper, Pi rich (witness-optimal policies in Pi), and gamma<=alpha+1: c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma)} (log n)^p, with r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}) and r_feas the derived plain-clipped-AIPW exponent. Moreover r_feas<=r_star, with equality at gamma=0 and more generally whenever kappa zeta(a,c,gamma)>=r_star(alpha,gamma). The strict positive gap occurs precisely in the nuisance-limited weak-overlap branch kappa zeta(a,c,gamma)<r_star(alpha,gamma); feasible tightness is resolved in the equal-exponent branch and remains open in the strict-gap branch (oeq:feasible-tight).
proposed_statement_changes.reason: The displayed two-sided rate bracket is proved. The informal clause saying the gap is positive for every gamma>0 is too strong under the corrected def:feasible-rate: if the nuisance exponents are strong enough that kappa zeta(a,c,gamma)>=r_star(alpha,gamma), then r_feas=min{r_star,kappa zeta}=r_star even when gamma>0. The tightness statement must therefore be localized to the strict-gap branch rather than all weak-overlap regimes.

open_obligations.what_is_open: Determine the exact attainable welfare-regret exponent in the strict weak-overlap nuisance-limited branch gamma>0 with kappa zeta(a,c,gamma)<r_star(alpha,gamma). Equivalently, prove either an improved feasible achievability result at exponent r_star(alpha,gamma), or a feasible-procedure lower bound showing that the weak-arm nuisance-learning cost forces a strictly slower exponent.
open_obligations.obstruction: The established thm:rate-characterization gives two nonmatching facts in this branch: the information-theoretic minimax converse M_n(alpha,gamma)>=c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} over all measurable Pi-valued estimators, and the explicit cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM upper bound of order n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma)}(log n)^p with r_feas<r_star. The converse is not a lower bound for a narrower formal class of 'genuinely feasible' procedures, because M_n already ranges over all measurable estimators. Conversely, the feasible upper analysis contains the clipped-region nuisance drift from lem:clip-bias; the present dependencies do not show whether that term is intrinsic or a proof artifact.
open_obligations.attempted: Using oeq:feasible-upper and thm:minimax-lower closes the problem whenever r_feas=r_star, as recorded in lem:feasible-attainment-at-equal-exponents. In the strict-gap branch this route gives only c n^{-r_star} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) and sup_P E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C n^{-r_feas}(log n)^p. Inferring a slower feasible lower bound from the minimax converse fails because r_star is the sharper lower-bound exponent and applies to all estimators, not a restricted feasible subclass. Inferring r_star-achievability for the cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM fails because the current drift bound still contains the weak-arm nuisance-estimation term.
open_obligations.partial_result: The exact feasible tightness question is solved in all equal-exponent regimes r_feas(alpha,gamma)=r_star(alpha,gamma), including gamma=0 and any gamma>0 with kappa zeta(a,c,gamma)>=r_star(alpha,gamma): the cross-fitted clipped-AIPW ERM attains n^{-r_star} up to logarithmic factors, and thm:minimax-lower rules out any faster polynomial exponent. In the remaining strict weak-overlap nuisance-limited branch, the strongest proved statement is the bracket c n^{-r_star(alpha,gamma)} <= M_n(alpha,gamma) <= sup_P E_P R_P(pi_hat_n) <= C n^{-r_feas(alpha,gamma)}(log n)^p with r_feas<r_star.
